etymology.description: semantic 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") + phonetic 甲(甲) (jiǎ)
etymology.interpretation: Combines 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth"), indicating an action involving the mouth, with 甲(甲) (jiǎ) for its sound, to represent the action of sipping or drinking a small amount.

examples.chinese: 他轻轻地呷了一口茶(他輕輕地呷了一口茶)。
examples.pinyin: Tā qīngqīng de xiā le yī kǒu chá.
examples.english: He took a gentle sip of tea.

examples.chinese: 她呷了一口咖啡(她呷了一口咖啡)，然后继续阅读(然後繼續閱讀)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xiā le yī kǒu kāfēi, ránhòu jìxù yuèdú.
examples.english: She took a sip of coffee, then continued reading.

examples.chinese: 这汤太烫了(這湯太燙了)，只能呷一小口(只能呷一小口)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè tāng tài tàng le, zhǐ néng xiā yī xiǎo kǒu.
examples.english: This soup is too hot, can only sip a little.
